name: Pan Fried or Sauteed Yellow Perch
recipeInstructions:
1. Dress, scale, and gut the fish, then pat dry. Season with salt and pepper.
2. Dredge the fish in a mixture of cornmeal and meal-flour.
3. Heat the fat until it is hot enough to cook at once but not burning or sputtering.
4. Place the fish in the skillet. Reduce heat to a barely medium flame and begin to brown the fish.
5. Keep the fish loose from the pan bottom with a thin metal spatula for 8-10 minutes per side, reducing heat if necessary to prevent frying too quickly.
6. Brown the fish until it is crisp and opaque; do not overcook.
7. Add 4 tablespoons butter to the skillet after removing the fish. Squeeze in the juice of one lemon (if using) and stir the fat and juices to loosen any remaining dredgings.
8. Pour the butter and lemon mixture over the browned fish.
9. VARIATION: After removing the fish, toss about 1/3 cup chopped black walnuts with the remaining butter until lightly browned. Sprinkle over the fish before serving.
